servicetitan.name: ServiceTitan

pricing.notes: Enterprise focus, requires annual contract, minimum tech count varies

servicetitan.value_proposition: All-in-one field service management platform for residential and commercial contractors. Covers dispatching, invoicing, marketing, reporting, and phone tracking.
weaknesses: Very expensive — out of reach for small shops (1-5 techs)
Complex setup with long onboarding (weeks to months)
Locked into annual contracts with steep cancellation fees
Overkill for contractors who just need phone/call management
Customer support complaints about slow response times
Steep learning curve for non-tech-savvy owners
common_complaints: Too expensive for what we use
We only use 20% of the features
Price keeps going up at renewal
servicetitan.nevermiss_positioning: NeverMiss solves the missed-call problem at a fraction of ServiceTitan's cost without requiring a complex platform overhaul. Works alongside ServiceTitan for shops that already have it.

housecall_pro.name: Housecall Pro

pricing.notes: More affordable than ServiceTitan but still a full FSM platform

housecall_pro.value_proposition: Cloud-based field service management built for home service pros. Scheduling, dispatching, invoicing, and marketing in one platform.
weaknesses: Phone management is an afterthought — no true missed-call AI
Customer support quality has declined as they scaled
Features can be buggy after updates
Limited customization for specific trade workflows
Marketing tools are basic compared to dedicated solutions
Reporting limitations frustrate data-driven owners
common_complaints: App crashes and bugs after updates
housecall_pro.nevermiss_positioning: Housecall Pro manages jobs but lets calls slip through the cracks. NeverMiss ensures every call gets answered and booked, plugging HCP's biggest gap.

jobber.name: Jobber

pricing.notes: Popular with smaller contractors, strong SMB focus

jobber.value_proposition: Simple, affordable field service software for small home service businesses. Easy quoting, scheduling, invoicing, and client communication.
weaknesses: No real AI phone answering — missed calls still go to voicemail
Limited scalability for growing businesses
Basic reporting compared to ServiceTitan
No built-in marketing tools
Two-way texting only on highest tier
Integration ecosystem is limited
common_complaints: Outgrew it quickly as we hired more techs
Wish it had better phone management
Reporting is too basic for our needs
jobber.nevermiss_positioning: Jobber handles the job workflow but leaves the front door (the phone) unattended. NeverMiss is the AI receptionist that ensures no Jobber user ever loses a lead to a missed call.

podium.name: Podium

pricing.notes: Expensive for what it does, focused on reviews and messaging

podium.value_proposition: Centralized business messaging platform. Collects reviews, manages webchat, enables text marketing, and processes payments.
weaknesses: Very expensive for small contractors ($399+/month)
Focused on messaging/reviews, not actual phone answering
Long contracts (often 12-24 months)
Complex setup for simple needs
AI capabilities are limited to templated responses
Doesn't actually answer phone calls
common_complaints: Way too expensive for a small business
Locked into a long contract we regret
Too many features we don't use
podium.nevermiss_positioning: Podium manages text conversations but can't pick up the phone. NeverMiss handles the actual call — the moment that matters most — at a fraction of Podium's cost.

smith_ai.name: Smith.ai

pricing.notes: Per-call pricing gets expensive fast for busy contractors

smith_ai.value_proposition: Live virtual receptionists blended with AI. Human agents answer calls, qualify leads, book appointments, and integrate with CRMs.
weaknesses: Gets very expensive at volume ($7.50-10+/call)
Human receptionists aren't available truly 24/7 in practice
Hold times during peak hours
Receptionists don't know the contractor's business deeply
Inconsistent quality depending on which receptionist answers
Per-call model penalizes busy businesses
common_complaints: Costs way more than expected when calls picked up
Receptionists didn't know our services well
smith_ai.nevermiss_positioning: NeverMiss delivers always-on AI answering at a predictable flat rate. No per-call surprises, no hold times, no inconsistent quality. AI that actually learns the contractor's business.

ruby_receptionists.name: Ruby Receptionists

pricing.notes: Per-minute model, expensive at scale, focused on professional services

ruby_receptionists.value_proposition: Live virtual receptionist service with US-based receptionists. Professional call handling, message taking, and basic appointment scheduling.
weaknesses: Per-minute billing burns through minutes fast with chatty callers
Not specialized for home services/contracting
Limited after-hours coverage compared to AI
Receptionists can only follow simple scripts
No AI intelligence — purely human-operated
Minutes run out fast for busy shops
common_complaints: Blew through our minutes in the first two weeks
After-hours coverage is hit or miss
Not worth the cost for high call volume
ruby_receptionists.nevermiss_positioning: Ruby charges by the minute and runs out fast. NeverMiss is unlimited AI answering for a flat rate — no minute caps, no overages, true 24/7 coverage.

numa.name: Numa

pricing.notes: Automotive-focused originally, expanding into home services

numa.value_proposition: AI-powered phone answering and customer communication platform. Automatically texts back missed calls and provides basic AI phone answering.
weaknesses: Originally built for automotive dealerships, not home services
AI conversations are template-heavy and rigid
Can't handle complex booking scenarios (multi-service, scheduling nuance)
Limited integration with home service tools
Text-back approach feels impersonal to callers expecting a voice
AI doesn't deeply understand trade-specific terminology
common_complaints: AI responses felt robotic and generic
Couldn't handle our specific services properly
Built for car dealers, not contractors
Text-back isn't enough — customers want to talk
Integration with our FSM was clunky
numa.nevermiss_positioning: Numa texts back. NeverMiss actually converses. Our AI understands contractor businesses, handles complex scheduling, and delivers a genuine phone experience — not just an automated text.

hatch.name: Hatch

pricing.notes: Focused on outbound lead engagement and reactivation, not inbound call handling

hatch.value_proposition: AI-powered customer engagement platform for home services. Automates lead follow-up, appointment reminders, and re-engagement campaigns via text and email.
weaknesses: Focused on outbound, not inbound call handling
Doesn't answer live phone calls
Expensive for what is essentially automated text outreach
AI conversations are template-driven, not truly intelligent
Doesn't solve the core missed-call problem
Overlap with features already in most FSM platforms
common_complaints: Our FSM already does follow-up texts
Customers want to talk to someone, not get a text
hatch.nevermiss_positioning: Hatch chases leads after the fact. NeverMiss catches them in the moment — answering the call live so the lead never slips away in the first place.

vs_live_answering.headline: Always-on AI at a fraction of the cost
key_points: Flat-rate pricing vs per-call/per-minute billing that escalates unpredictably
True 24/7/365 coverage with zero hold times
AI that learns the specific business vs generic human scripts
No quality variance — consistent experience every call
Scales infinitely without cost spikes during busy seasons

vs_fsm_platforms.headline: Solve the missed-call problem without overhauling your entire stack
key_points: Works alongside existing FSM tools — no rip-and-replace
Fraction of the cost (no per-tech pricing)
Set up in minutes, not weeks
Focused on the #1 revenue leak: unanswered calls
No long-term contracts or complex onboarding

vs_basic_missed_call.headline: Smarter than text-back, cheaper than enterprise messaging
key_points: Actually answers the call with AI voice, not just texts back
Handles complex booking scenarios (multi-service, urgency routing)
Built specifically for home service contractors
Genuinely conversational AI vs templated robotic responses
